prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ONLY_DWT_catboost', seasonality_champion='DWT_rf', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: The validation folds reveal significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.0958) and high seasonality variance (1.0763), indicating models capture patterns very differently. ETS dominates as overall champion with best RMSE (96.24) while also excelling in both early and late horizons. FT-based models show poor seasonality capture (negative correlations), making them poor candidates for combination. The data-driven recommendation is DBA combination to leverage diverse model behaviors while mitigating disagreement through dynamic weighting.

IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "ONLY_DWT_catboost",
  "seasonality_champion": "DWT_rf",
  "overall_champion": "ETS",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "THETA", "late": "NaiveSeasonal"},
  "tier1_models": ["ETS", "THETA", "DWT_rf", "ARIMA", "rf", "DWT_catboost", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "NaiveSeasonal"],
  "tier2_models": ["FT_catboost", "DWT_svr", "ONLY_DWT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "CWT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "CWT_catboost", "FT_svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 1.0958,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["down", "down", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "The validation folds reveal significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.0958) and high seasonality variance (1.0763), indicating models capture patterns very differently. ETS dominates as overall champion with best RMSE (96.24) while also excelling in both early and late horizons. FT-based models show poor seasonality capture (negative correlations), making them poor candidates for combination. The data-driven recommendation is DBA combination to leverage diverse model behaviors while mitigating disagreement through dynamic weighting."
}
raw.skeptic: {"add_names": [], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "Current candidate set has good diversity: 1 baseline (dba_combination), 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k5), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35). No redundancy detected. All candidates are valid from universe. n_windows=3 ≥3 but current set already has diverse types. Score margin top2=0.0016 indicates tight competition among top candidates. No leakage issues: baselines never leak, selection candidates use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection, weighted candidate uses shrinkage-based weighting (anti-leakage). Universe leaderboards show best_single_by_validation performs well but current set already has sufficient diversity and performance.", "changes": [], "when_good": ""}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: The validation folds reveal significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.0958) and high seasonality variance (1.0763), indicating models capture patterns very differently. ETS dominates as overall champion with best RMSE (96.24) while also excelling in both early and late horizons. FT-based models show poor seasonality capture (negative correlations), making them poor candidates for combination. The data-driven recommendation is DBA combination to leverage diverse model behaviors while mitigating disagreement through dynamic weighting.

proposer.rationale: Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high seasonality variance (1.0763) and model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.0958). ETS is overall champion with RMSE=97.60 vs worst ONLY_FT_rf at 207.29. n_unique_winners=7 suggests per-horizon selection diversity. inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35 provides dynamic weighting with shrinkage=0.35 to mitigate overfitting on only 3 windows. topk_mean_per_horizon_k5 adds robustness by averaging top-5 models per horizon.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has good diversity: 1 baseline (dba_combination), 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k5), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35). No redundancy detected. All candidates are valid from universe. n_windows=3 ≥3 but current set already has diverse types. Score margin top2=0.0016 indicates tight competition among top candidates. No leakage issues: baselines never leak, selection candidates use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection, weighted candidate uses shrinkage-based weighting (anti-leakage). Universe leaderboards show best_single_by_validation performs well but current set already has sufficient diversity and performance.
